title: veronica barrelieri subsp. andrasovszkyi
intro: Veronica barrelieri subsp. andrasovszkyi, also known as Hungarian speedwell, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which make it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Hungarian speedwell include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate coughs and colds, and support immune function. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
